invariants: Keep source provenance distinct: command-line values, environment values, prompts, default_map, and declared defaults should remain distinguishable through ParameterSource.
Treat envvar values as external string input before conversion; do not assume Python boolean identity from environment text.
For flags, separate user activation from declared default behavior; flag_value is the activated value, while defaults and default_map still follow default semantics.
When comparing defaults with flag values, avoid invoking arbitrary callable objects just to establish equality or display text.
For multiple declarations sharing one destination parameter, evaluate each option's default behavior before collapsing to the final parameter value.
CliRunner.invoke should isolate Click-managed stdin, stdout, stderr, cwd, env, and exception capture without leaking hidden verifier files into the run workspace.
Synthetic stdin must behave enough like a readable text stream for file parameters, prompts, echoing, and EOF handling.
If a helper temporarily patches global stream-related state, restoration after invoke is part of the behavior contract.
Result.output, stdout_bytes, stderr_bytes, return_value, exception, and exit_code should be checked according to the invoked command's standalone_mode behavior.
Prompt rendering combines option metadata, default display, confirmation behavior, hidden input, and terminal stream capabilities.
Visible default text can differ from the internal default value; keep display-only strings out of conversion logic.
Terminal width, color stripping, hidden prompts, and pager/progress output should be tested through Click helpers rather than raw print assumptions.
ParamType.convert owns user-visible conversion errors; Option and Argument should not duplicate type-specific validation when conversion can handle it.
Composite and repeated parameters require normalization at the boundary between parser output and type conversion.
Path and File types cross filesystem, stream, and lazy-open behavior; tests should cover value, exception, and cleanup semantics.
